purpose: QBER-vs-Im_bias correlation test on Fez/TOKEN2 (5 sweeps)
rationale: If Im_bias is noise-driven (hardware decoherence), it should correlate with the Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBER) of a reference circuit. If Im_bias is algorithm-driven (ansatz depth, circuit compilation), QBER and Im_bias should be uncorrelated.

H_Noise_Driven.implication: Bias is hardware-decoherence driven; should be reducible by error mitigation

H_Bias_Driven.description: Im_bias does NOT correlate with QBER (|rho| < 0.3)
H_Bias_Driven.implication: Bias is ansatz-/algorithm-driven; irreducible by error mitigation

H_Qber_Sanity_Statevector.implication: QBER is a meaningful noise indicator (sanity check)

decision_rule: PASS H_Qber_Sanity_Statevector if statevector QBER == 0 exactly.
On QPU: measure QBER on a |00> reference circuit and Im_bias for the same 5 theta_values as pt_im_bias_token2_results.json.
Compute Pearson rho(QBER, Im_bias) on the 5 points.
qpu_protocol: 5 sequential 1-Pub jobs on Fez/TOKEN2: each job runs (a) the ansatz on H_imag and (b) a |00> reference circuit. 4096 shots each. Plus 1 statevector baseline run (no QPU cost).
